sections.content:
SPACE didn't work - the level didn't complete. Let me check what changed after pressing SPACE. The board_changed is true, so something did change. Let me look at the segmentation to see what happened.
Actually, looking at the images, they all look the same. The step counter went from 250 to 254, but the board looks identical. Let me check if the black F moved or if something else changed.
Let me reconsider the goal. Maybe I need to overlap ALL THREE F shapes, not just two. The charcoal F is at cols 0-8, while the yellow and black F are at cols 51-62. I need to move the charcoal F to overlap them too.
But wait - I don't know which key controls the charcoal F. Let me check if LEFT/RIGHT controls the charcoal F instead of the black F.
Actually, let me re-examine. The black F moved from the left side to the right side (overlapping the yellow F) when I pressed RIGHT repeatedly. So RIGHT moves the black F. Maybe I need to move the charcoal F too?
Let me check the current state more carefully.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
nodes = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        minr = min(p[0] for p in b)
        maxr = max(p[0] for p in b)
        minc = min(p[1] for p in b)
        maxc = max(p[1] for p in b)
        print(f"color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} hash={n['hash']} id={n['id']} bbox=[{minr},{minc}]-[{maxr},{maxc}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Let me reconsider. Maybe the goal isn't to overlap F shapes. Let me look at the level more carefully. I see:

3. A black F on the right (cols 54-62)
Wait, maybe I need to move the charcoal F to overlap the yellow F, not the black F? Or maybe I need to move the charcoal F all the way to the right side?
Actually, let me reconsider the whole game. The black F was initially on the left side (same position as the charcoal F now). I moved it right to overlap the yellow F. But that didn't complete the level.
Maybe the goal is to move the CHARCOAL F to overlap the yellow F? The black F might be a "wrong" piece or a decoy. Let me try moving the charcoal F right.
But wait, how do I control the charcoal F? RIGHT moved the black F. Maybe I need to select the charcoal F first? Or maybe different keys control different objects?
Let me try pressing UP or DOWN to see if that selects a different object or moves the charcoal F.
